summary: PR 2541 advanced matching for sysdolphin baselib function hsd_803A949C, changing the implementation and header signature. The diff replaces a local CardStateExt/CardCmdEntry approach with CardState field usage and command-buffer access macros, adjusts control flow and data checks across CARD close/read/verify cases, and updates the public prototype to void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1). Bot output reports hsd_803A949C improving from 58.78% to 93.40%, while the PR title says 92%.

key_files.role: Primary implementation changed for hsd_803A949C and helper types/macros.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c added 148 deleted 164 hunks 17
raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c replaces CardStateExt/CardCmdEntry with CardBufEntry and CMD_* macros
raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c changes hsd_803A949C from one argument to two arguments
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report for main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 hsd_803A949C 58.78% -> 93.40%

key_files.role: Header prototype updated to match recovered function signature.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.h added 1 deleted 1 hunks 1
raw_diff lines 546-551: void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) -> void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1)

what_changed: Changed extern hsd_804D7980 from s32 to volatile s32 in hsd_3A94.c.
Removed local CardStateExt and CardCmdEntry definitions and introduced a compact CardBufEntry type.
Removed hsd_803A949C_GetEntry and replaced entry field access with CMD_S32, CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR, and CMD_TYPE macros over hsd_804D1138 plus the volatile hsd_804D7980 index.
Changed helpers hsd_803A949C_Close and hsd_803A949C_IconSize to take CardState* and use named CardState fields such as file_info and digest.
Changed hsd_803A949C signature from void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) to void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1), and updated the header prototype.
Reworked command-case logic to use CMD_* command-buffer reads, direct CardState fields, explicit NULL checks for copy destinations, and repeated digest/update calls through CMD_STATE.
Added a bounded for loop before assigning state->x20 from state->file_info.fileNo.
In case 13, replaced some CardBlock pointer use with direct byte indexing into state->x0 for file id/version extraction.
Bot report records hsd_803A949C score improvement from 58.78% to 93.40%.

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header when the implementation signature changed, avoiding a stale prototype.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c changes hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) to hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1)
raw_diff lines 550-551: hsd_3A94.h prototype updated

smart_moves.text: Moved several accesses from an ad hoc local CardStateExt layout to the existing CardState type and named fields, improving type fidelity for known structure members.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CARDClose(&state->xC) -> CARDClose(&state->file_info)
raw_diff: state->x430 digest accesses -> CMD_STATE->digest accesses

smart_moves.text: Centralized command-buffer indexing behind CMD_* macros instead of repeating hand-expanded pointer expressions throughout the function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: introduces CMD_S32, CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR, CMD_TYPE
raw_diff: switch(entry->x0) -> switch(CMD_TYPE)

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function signature changes, update the matching header in the same PR so call surfaces remain truthful.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implementation signature changed to void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1)
raw_diff lines 550-551: header prototype changed to the same two-argument signature

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prefer real recovered struct fields over temporary duplicate local layouts when the underlying type is known.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removes CardStateExt
raw_diff: uses CardState* in hsd_803A949C_Close and hsd_803A949C_IconSize
raw_diff: state->file_info and CMD_STATE->digest replace offset-style fields xC and x430 in several places

naming_conventions.name: CardBufEntry
naming_conventions.observation: Introduced as a compact local type for command-buffer slots while retaining unknown field names x0/x4/etc.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct CardBufEntry { s32 x0, x4, x8, xC; ... }

naming_conventions.name: CMD_S32 / CMD_STATE / CMD_PTR / CMD_TYPE
naming_conventions.observation: Uppercase local macros describe command-buffer access patterns and distinguish scalar, pointer, state, and type access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: defines CMD_S32, CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR, CMD_TYPE

naming_conventions.name: chan
naming_conventions.observation: The newly added first parameter is named chan in both the implementation and header; the diff does not show it being used in the excerpt.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1)
raw_diff lines 550-551: header prototype uses s32 chan

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use volatile on the command-buffer index global hsd_804D7980.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: extern s32 hsd_804D7980 -> extern volatile s32 hsd_804D7980
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is a matching tactic visible in the diff, but the PR evidence does not include an explanation or adjacent regression evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace an entry pointer local with macro-expanded command-buffer reads at different offsets.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removes hsd_803A949C_GetEntry
raw_diff: introduces CMD_S32(off) over hsd_804D1138 + off indexed by hsd_804D7980
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The macros likely affect generated access shape. The PR evidence supports that they improved the function score, but does not prove they are a general style preference.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a bounded empty loop before reading file_info.fileNo.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (state->file_info.fileNo != -1) break; } state->x20 = state->file_info.fileNo;
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The loop is evidence of a matching/control-flow change in this function only; no validation details explain why the bound 10 is required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Duplicate verification blocks inside each branch instead of sharing a common post-branch block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: file id, x12, and memcmp checks are moved inside both offset and non-offset branches in one case
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This likely changes control-flow shape for matching. Treat as local evidence, not a global recommendation.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting hsd_3A94 command-buffer access pattern
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2541 replaces a CardCmdEntry pointer model with CMD_S32/CMD_STATE/CMD_PTR/CMD_TYPE macros over hsd_804D1138 plus hsd_804D7980. If later PRs confirm this is the correct command-buffer layout, it may merit a local source comment, typed wrapper, or path fact for sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3A94.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: defines CMD_S32(off) using hsd_804D1138 + off and hsd_804D7980
raw_diff: switch(entry->x0) -> switch(CMD_TYPE)
raw_diff: state = ((CardState*) CMD_S32(0x14))
source_update_candidates.reason: The pattern is central to the PR and may be reusable, but this single slice lacks enough explanation to promote a canonical layout or global convention.

rejection_notes: Do not promote volatile hsd_804D7980 as a general matching tactic; the PR shows the edit but gives no rationale or regression evidence.
Do not treat CMD_* macros as a global standard; they are local evidence for hsd_3A94 only.
Do not claim a fully matched function; the title and bot report indicate partial match percentages, not 100%.
Do not infer human reviewer intent; no review comments are present.
